_meta.purpose: Mor-category-level prompt instructions plumbed into the four ETF analysis prompts (Past Returns / Cost & Team / Risk / Future Outlook) as `categoryInstructions`. The same bullet list renders in all four prompts when the fund's Mor category has an entry here.
_meta.coverageRule: Populated for Mor categories with approximately >=50 US-listed ETFs as of 2026-05. Categories below the threshold render no category-specific block — the per-factor group instructions cover them.
_meta.instructionStyle: Each category carries 6 bullets total — 3 `topQualities` describing what separates the top decile / quintile of ETFs from the rest in that category (the positives to look for), and 3 `watchOuts` describing category-specific risks and traps a retail reader should be wary of (the negatives to flag). Each bullet is 1-2 lines max — do not pad.
_meta.keyConvention: Keys MUST be exact Mor category names from etf-analysis-categories.json -> categories[].name. Unknown keys are silently ignored.

topQualities: Tracking error vs the named benchmark — top funds hold within ~5 bps and consistently deliver index-grade performance year after year.
Cost discipline — top decile is 0.03-0.05% (VOO, VTI, IVV), letting compounding work uninterrupted across decades.
Tax efficiency — top funds report near-zero realised capital-gain distributions over rolling 5Y; in-kind redemption discipline is best-in-class here.
watchOuts: Persistent tracking drift above ~25 bps is a Fail signal for a 'passive' fund — usually a fee waiver expired or the basket is drifting from the index.
Sub-$200M Large Blend ETFs hide retail liquidity friction even when headline AUM looks stable; spreads tax round-trips.
One-off cap-gain payouts above ~0.5% of NAV signal weak in-kind redemption discipline — uncommon at scale but the warning sign in smaller Large Blend funds.

topQualities: Style purity — top funds load on growth (P/E, EPS-growth) versus the named index, so the bet retail signed up for is the bet they actually own.
Long-run alpha vs Russell 1000 Growth — top funds match or beat the benchmark net of fees over 5Y / 10Y windows.
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.06% (VUG, IUSG, SCHG), giving up no compounding to fee drag.
watchOuts: Top-10 concentration — passive cap-weighted Large Growth can hit 55-60% in mega-cap tech without an explicit cap, effectively a sector bet in disguise.
Style drift into blend or quality territory hides the tilt; retail ends up paying growth-fund fees for blend-fund exposure.
Above ~0.30% expense without a clear active mandate rarely net-beats low-cost peers consistently — most active large-growth funds don't.

topQualities: True value tilt — top funds hold lower P/B and higher dividend yield than the S&P 500, so the value premise is in the actual basket.
Dividend stability — for dividend-tilt members, top funds show multi-year consecutive distribution growth (Aristocrats-style discipline).
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.08% (VTV, IUSV, SCHV) and 10Y CAGR sits within tracking tolerance of Russell 1000 Value.
watchOuts: A fund whose sector weights mirror the broad market is mis-labelled as 'value' — check actual sector tilts versus the index, not just the name.
Flat or cut distributions in the dividend-tilt sub-set signal that the underlying portfolio's payout health is deteriorating.
Lagging Russell 1000 Value by >100 bps annualised over 10Y is a style-purity issue, not a style headwind — different problems.

topQualities: Size purity — top funds stay inside the Russell Midcap (or S&P 400) defined size band, delivering the mid-cap premium retail came for.
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.05% (VO, IJH, SCHM); fee inflation in mid-cap is rarely justified.
AUM + liquidity — top funds run $5B+ with $50M+ daily volume and cents-tight spreads even outside large-cap.
watchOuts: Drift down into small-cap or up into large-cap dilutes the mid-cap tilt and means the fund is no longer doing the job it was bought for.
Mid-cap spreads widen materially below ~$200M AUM and bite retail round-trips invisibly.
Weaker in-kind redemption discipline shows up as larger annual cap-gain distributions versus large-cap peers — check the 5Y record.

topQualities: Growth purity — top funds load on the actual P/E and EPS-growth screens versus the named index, capturing the mid-cap-growth premium.
Size purity (Russell Midcap / S&P 400 band) — top funds resist the gravitational pull up into large-cap mega-cap tech.
Cost — top decile is 0.05-0.08% (IJK, VOT); broad mid-cap-growth needs no active mandate to deliver.
watchOuts: Drift up into large-cap mega-cap tech is the most common bottom-quintile signal — check the top-10 weights against the index.
Runaway top-10 concentration in a single mid-cap name flags either a closet single-stock bet or an imminent large-cap migration.
Above ~0.40% expense ratio needs an active mandate; most active mid-cap-growth funds don't beat passive net of fees.

topQualities: Value purity — top funds load on lower P/B and higher dividend yield versus the named index in a size band where value historically pays off.
Size purity (Russell Midcap / S&P 400 band) — top funds avoid drifting down into small-cap where the value factor behaves differently.
Dividend stability — top funds in the dividend-tilt sub-set show multi-year distribution growth.
watchOuts: Drift down into small-cap is the most common tell — small-cap-value drawdowns are materially deeper than mid-cap-value, retail isn't pricing that.
Sector weights that mirror broad mid-cap (not value-skewed) mean the fund is mis-labelled.
Above ~0.40% expense ratio needs an active mandate to justify against low-cost passive peers.

topQualities: Size purity — top funds stay inside the Russell 2000 (or S&P 600) defined size band, capturing the small-cap premium retail came for.
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.06% (VB, IJR, SCHA); broad small-cap needs no active mandate.
AUM + liquidity — top funds run $5B+ with tight spreads despite the harder small-cap microstructure.
watchOuts: Drift down into micro-cap is a hidden-risk tell that amplifies drawdowns beyond what retail expects from a 'small-cap' label.
Small-cap spreads can widen materially under ~$200M AUM and tax retail round-trips invisibly.
Persistent tracking drift above ~30 bps is a Fail; small-cap ETFs miss the index more than large-cap, but not by that much in top funds.

topQualities: Growth purity — top funds load on the actual growth-defining screen (EPS, sales growth) versus the named index, not just 'growthy names'.
Size purity (Russell 2000 / S&P 600 band) — top funds stay inside the defined small-cap band where the growth premium lives.
Cost — top decile is 0.06-0.10% (IJT, VBK); broad passive small-growth at near-equity-fund prices.
watchOuts: Drift down into micro-cap can mask the worst-year drawdown — micro-cap growth amplifies broad-market drawdowns sharply.
Drift up into mid-cap means the fund is no longer doing the job retail bought.
Worst calendar-year drawdown 5+ pp deeper than the benchmark signals concentration or size drift, not just market beta.

topQualities: Value purity — top funds load on lower P/B and higher dividend yield versus the named index in a size band where value historically pays off.
Size purity (Russell 2000 / S&P 600 band) — top funds resist drift down into micro-cap.
Cost — top decile is 0.06-0.10% for passive (IJS, VBR), ~0.25% for actively-tilted small-value (AVUV).
watchOuts: Drift down into micro-cap can mask the worst-year drawdown — small-cap value drew down ~35% in 2020 intraday.
Sector weights that mirror broad small-cap (not value-skewed) mean the fund is mis-labelled.
Above ~0.40% expense without an active mandate is hard to justify against AVUV-style active or low-cost passive peers.

topQualities: Currency-hedge discipline — top funds clearly state hedged vs unhedged and don't switch mid-life.
Country / region weights match the named index disclosure tightly, so retail knows exactly the basket they own.
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.07% (IXUS, VEA, SCHF) — foreign equity at near-domestic-passive prices.
watchOuts: A hedge that's 'sometimes on' is a backtest, not a strategy — flag any fund with a shifting hedge policy.
Foreign withholding is mostly unrecoverable in tax-advantaged accounts; top funds disclose the drag, weaker ones bury it.
Closed-market premium / discount discipline matters — funds without active market-makers show large mid-day premiums during European / Asian hours.

topQualities: Single-country concentration cap — top funds explicitly cap China + Taiwan combined below ~30-35% so EM stays diversified.
Cost — top decile is 0.07-0.11% (IEMG, VWO, SCHE), capturing the EM premium without paying active-EM fees.
Liquidity in stress — top funds run $5B+ with tight spreads even during EM open-hours mismatch.
watchOuts: Cap-weighted EM ETFs without single-country caps can run 50-60% in 2-3 names, turning a 'diversified' sleeve into a concentrated country bet.
Mixed-hedge or shifting-hedge funds usually backfit performance; currency-hedge policy must be clearly stated and stable.
Smaller EM ETFs get marked off NAV materially during stress windows when the underlying markets are closed.

topQualities: Sub-sector breakdown clarity — top funds disclose software vs semis vs internet weights so retail knows the exact tech-cycle exposure.
Cost — top decile is 0.08-0.10% (XLK, VGT, FTEC); passive sector tech needs no premium fee.
Calendar-year alignment with Nasdaq 100 — top funds track the broad tech cycle directionally without hidden sub-sector overshoots.
watchOuts: Top-10 concentration at 60-70% means the fund is essentially a mega-cap tech bet; equal-weight or tier-2 variants are different products.
Mixing software + semis + internet without disclosure overlaps with retail's Large Growth holdings unintentionally.
Above ~0.50% expense needs a thematic / niche justification — broad-tech ETFs at that fee don't outperform low-cost peers.

topQualities: Pure-play equity-REIT focus — top funds hold >=90% US-listed equity REITs, delivering the REIT sleeve cleanly.
Dividend stability — top funds show multi-year consecutive distribution growth, signalling tenant + debt health in the portfolio.
Cost — top decile is 0.08-0.12% (VNQ, USRT, SCHH); broad REIT exposure at near-passive prices.
watchOuts: Mortgage REITs, REOCs, and real-estate-adjacent stocks in the basket dilute the equity-REIT exposure retail signed up for.
A REIT distribution cut signals underlying tenant or debt stress; treat it as an early-warning indicator, not a one-off.
REITs are interest-rate-sensitive — 2022's rate shock drew the category down ~25-30%; outsized losses signal concentration or sub-sector issues.

topQualities: Sub-sector exposure clarity — top funds are explicit about broad health (pharma + biotech + devices + services) vs pharma-pure vs biotech-only.
Cost — top decile is 0.08-0.10% (XLV, VHT, IYH); passive broad health needs no premium fee.
Multi-year peer rank — top funds stay in the top two quartiles across 1Y / 3Y / 5Y windows.
watchOuts: Top-10 concentration above ~40% in a broad-health fund signals hidden pharma-mega-cap concentration; pure biotech can run 50%+ structurally.
Pure biotech carries binary FDA-event risk; calendar-year drawdowns can be sharper than broad sector.
Bouncing between top and bottom quartile across cycles signals a hidden sub-sector tilt the label doesn't capture.

topQualities: Duration discipline — top funds hold ~5-7Y duration matching Bloomberg US Agg, delivering core-bond exposure cleanly.
Credit-quality mix — top funds hold ~70-80% Treasury + agency MBS + IG corporate, in line with the 'core' label.
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.05% (AGG, BND, SCHZ); core bond at near-zero fee drag.
watchOuts: Drift to 8+ years duration is a hidden rate bet retail does not sign up for in 'core' funds.
HY or EM debt sneaking into the 'core' basket without disclosure dilutes the label and adds credit risk retail isn't pricing.
2022 was a -13% benchmark year; outsized losses (deeper than -15%) signal credit or duration drift.

topQualities: Duration discipline — top funds hold 1-3Y, delivering low-duration income as advertised.
Credit-quality mix — top funds are Treasury + IG corporate, paying for short-duration risk only.
Cost — top decile is 0.03-0.10% (BSV, SHY, VGSH); short-term bond at low fee drag.
watchOuts: Drift to 4+ years duration is a hidden rate bet retail doesn't sign up for in 'short-term' funds.
Slipping into HY for yield is a tell — the headline yield isn't paid for by short-duration risk.
If the fund's yield is below HYSA net of fees, the only reason to hold is duration optionality if rates fall — not yield itself.

topQualities: Duration discipline — top funds hold under 1Y effective duration, behaving like cash with a small yield premium.
Yield vs HYSA — top funds clearly beat HYSA net of fees in a positive-rate environment.
Cost — top decile is 0.05-0.10% (BIL, SGOV, USFR); ultrashort at near-cash fees.
watchOuts: Anything above 1Y effective duration is misleading for an 'ultrashort' label — check the actual figure, not just the category.
CLO-AAA exposure above ~20% changes the risk character from Treasury-only ultrashort; not necessarily wrong, but not what the label implies.
Above ~0.20% expense is hard to justify in ultrashort — fees eat directly into the small yield premium over cash.

topQualities: Credit-quality discipline — top funds hold >=85% A-rated and above, delivering IG corporate exposure cleanly.
Duration discipline — match the named benchmark, so retail's rate exposure is what they signed up for.
Cost — top decile is 0.04-0.10% (LQD, VCIT, USIG); IG corporate at low fee drag.
watchOuts: BBB tilt-down matters materially in credit-stress years — funds heavier in BBB lose more in stress than the headline IG label suggests.
Large duration drift away from the named benchmark hides a rate call retail isn't expecting.
2022 saw IG corporate down ~13-18%; outsized losses signal long-duration drift or low-BBB concentration.

topQualities: Credit + sector disclosure — top funds publish CCC % and sector concentration plainly so retail knows the actual risk taken.
Yield spread vs IG — top funds' spread above IG is in line with peers; the yield is paid for measured credit risk.
Cost — top decile is 0.15-0.25% (HYG, JNK, USHY); broad HY exposure at fee levels appropriate for the credit work.
watchOuts: CCC >15% or single-sector concentration above ~25% (often energy) is concentration risk the headline yield doesn't fully price.
Outsized headline yield versus peers means outsized credit risk, not free income.
2008 drew HY down -25 to -30% and 2020 intra-year -20%; outsized losses signal CCC tier or sector concentration.

topQualities: Duration discipline — match the named benchmark; top funds deliver intermediate-muni rate exposure cleanly.
Credit-quality mix — top funds hold predominantly A-rated and above, delivering tax-exempt income with low default risk.
Tax-equivalent-yield (TEY) disclosure — top funds quote TEY at ~32% federal (plus state exemption for in-state holders) so retail can compare to taxable peers.
watchOuts: BBB or non-rated tilt-down matters in stress windows — flag any fund whose credit-quality mix has slipped over time.
AMT-bond exposure should be near zero for plain Muni National Interm — funds with material AMT % matter for high-income retail.
Above ~0.30% expense in IG muni rarely justifies itself versus low-cost passive peers (MUB, VTEB, TFI at 0.05-0.10%).

topQualities: Daily-tracking quality — top funds hit ~3x (or stated multiple) of the underlying daily move within tight tolerance.
AUM + dollar volume — top funds run $5-25B with billions in daily volume, supporting the short-term-trading thesis they exist for.
Cost discipline — top decile is 0.95-1.00% (TQQQ, SPXL, SOXL); fees above ~1.20% pay for nothing extra in this category.
watchOuts: Daily-reset path dependency means actual return over multi-month windows is meaningfully below stated multiple × underlying — these are NEVER buy-and-hold.
Below ~$500M AUM these become unusable for the trading thesis they're sold for — spreads widen and execution costs eat any directional edge.
Anything sold to buy-and-hold retail is mis-labelled; the fund is a short-term tactical tool, not a portfolio holding.

topQualities: Daily-tracking quality — top funds hit stated inverse multiple within tight tolerance.
AUM + dollar volume — top funds run $1-5B with high daily volume, supporting tactical hedging use.
Cost discipline — top decile is 0.85-0.95% (SQQQ, SDS, SPXS); fees above ~1.20% are hard to justify.
watchOuts: Compounding decay erodes inverse funds faster than leveraged-long peers in flat or mildly-up markets — never a portfolio holding.
Sub-$200M inverse funds are essentially un-tradable for the short-term hedging use case — spreads and execution costs dominate.
Retail using inverse ETFs as a buy-and-hold 'protection' is bottom-quintile usage — these are tactical tools only.

topQualities: Top funds show stable or slightly-rising NAV alongside their distribution yield — true income, not capital being returned dressed as yield.
Top funds explicitly disclose option structure, % overwritten, DTE, strike methodology, roll discipline, and notional coverage ratio.
Top funds also disclose ROC share in distributions and how the strategy behaved across low-vol and high-vol regimes.
watchOuts: A steadily declining price-only NAV next to positive total return signals structural NAV erosion — the distribution is partly your own capital.
High return-of-capital (ROC) % in distributions is the fund returning your money back as 'yield' — check the year-by-year ROC share.
Option-premium income is less tax-efficient than qualified dividends in taxable accounts — the headline yield is materially smaller after tax.

topQualities: Top funds make it explicit that the buffer + cap are only realised at outcome-period end, so retail understands the holding-period commitment.
Top funds clearly disclose buffer vs floor structure (buffer absorbs first-loss layer; floor caps losses at a fixed level) and the cap-reset methodology.
Top laddered series spread outcome periods so retail isn't locked into a single cap window — entry-timing risk is diluted.
watchOuts: Mid-period buyers / sellers get a completely different exposure than the headline buffer + cap — anyone trading these mid-period is mis-using them.
Active-ratchet / dynamic-reset funds need to disclose the reset trigger clearly; opaque resets are a bottom-quintile tell.
Fees above ~1.00% are structurally paying option-spread + admin overhead (category norm is 0.65-0.85%).

topQualities: Top funds disclose how puts are financed (paid outright, financed by selling calls, or built via spreads), so retail knows the trade-off.
Top funds maintain laddered or rolling hedges so protection is always in place — no gap periods between hedge expirations.
Top funds' historical drawdowns landed near the stated buffer / floor — the hedge worked when it was needed.
watchOuts: Hidden hedge financing is a bottom-quintile tell — if you can't tell how the protection is paid for, you can't price it.
'Full upside with downside protection' is overselling — top funds disclose realistic bull-market lag (the cost of insurance).
Fees above ~1.00% need to justify the hedge construction premium (category norm is 0.50-0.85%).
